posts.body_text:
Completely agree.
Having worked with traditional RPA on 50+ sites and more recently production-scale scraping across 250+ sites, the brittleness difference is very real.
posts.stimulus: Impact of AI on automation tools

posts.body_text: Iffat Hafsa Absolutely. These shifts have been disruptive. At the same time, I’m already seeing many people come back stronger as they adapt and work with AI, which really reinforces why staying in touch with technology matters.

Fully agree.
SaaS will just absorb AI by adding agentic layers so users can access features via prompts instead of complex workflows.
posts.stimulus: Evolution of SaaS with AI agents

posts.body_text:
I’m doubling down on checker skills, catching bugs, missing constraints, and edge cases AI introduces due to incomplete prompts.
 AI can write code; engineers must protect outcomes.
posts.stimulus: Engineers' roles in the AI landscape

posts.body_text: Alain V. Tools speed up coding. Teams deliver outcomes. We position ourselves as the execution team that leverages these tools to ship things reliably.
Same realization here. The hype said agents would replace teams, but in reality AI works best when it supports people.
Totally agree, Harsh Kumar — AI really shines only in the hands of experienced people.
posts.stimulus: Dependence of AI on human expertise

posts.body_text: Congratulations! If you ever need remote n8n experts to support your automation roadmap, our team would be glad to assist.
Congratulations on the success of Phase One! Best wishes to Eli and look forward to hearing about your next venture.

Absolutely, Oleksandra Bielova , I completely agree. Many teams take up automation just to show progress, even when it’s not really needed. Often it’s more about keeping leadership happy than solving a real problem.
This really resonates, I made a similar move early in my career and started my own agency, would be great to connect and see if there’s an opportunity to create a win-win collaboration.
AI isn’t about disappearing people, it’s about making them superstars at what they do best!
AI is a great tool, but if we stop thinking ourselves, it will end up replacing us
posts.stimulus: Critical thinking in the age of AI

posts.body_text: Fatima Yusuf, We also developed a similar Virtual Try On Feature for a store. We can exchange our thoughts / insights in the LinkedIn Message Box
Agentic AI takes AI to the next level by learning, adapting, and making smart decisions to boost business and customer experiences
Jonathan Coulter Exactly! Search today is about being findable across AI, agents, and humans. That’s why we focus on clear, factual, and crawlable content that shows up wherever your audience asks

Absolutely Ben Edwards I think boutique firms can really shine here because they can give very niche, specific answers to unique problems

Great post Panagiotis Spiliotopoulos! AI in pharma can really help make work faster and more accurate. I like your idea about AI agents working together like a team, it feels like the future!

At CodingRippler, we build AI tools like Sales Rep Assistants and smart automations. Would love to connect and see how we can work together on your AI projects

So true Kyri Chrysandreas! Too much data can create confusion instead of clarity. At CodingRippler, we help businesses focus on the right metrics by building smart dashboards and automation, so leaders can make quick, confident decisions without drowning in numbers

Hi Trent Gillespie,
Sounds like a great opportunity! I help companies build and launch real AI solutions, not just hype. Would love to connect and explore how we can work together

Totally agree! Ojassvi Pradhan,

At Coding Rippler, we’ve seen how AI isn’t just improving existing products but creating completely new opportunities. It’s exciting to help businesses build solutions that customers didn’t even know they needed and then watch those ideas turn into whole new markets!

Totally agree! In startups, things are always moving fast and it can feel messy. 

At CodingRippler , we help startups do just that automating workflows and building smart solutions so teams can focus on moving fast with confidence!
Great insights! Choosing the right way to test LLMs can make or break AI success

Seeing things from both sides really helps you grow and solve problems better.

We follow the same at CodingRippler and it has helped us build stronger solutions for our clients
Thanks Jeff. I think the team at Infor (specially you, Mesfin and Bill) helped me build a strong foundation and I owe it to you guys. Let's catchup sometime :)

posts.body_text: Shreyash Gupta Thanks so much! I've been very happy to see your great success over the last few years - keep on doing what you're doing!
Thanks Jeff. I think the team at Infor (specially you, Mesfin and Bill) helped me build a strong foundation and I owe it to you guys. Let's catchup sometime :)

Shreyash Gupta I'd love to catch up one of these days, it's been too long! I'll message you and we can go from there.

We had a heck of a team at Infor, didn't we? I'm so glad that we were able to help you get to where you are now - and I feel the same way. Aketh T.M No pre-built Windows binaries yet. You can install Node.js, then: git clone && cd merged-polygpt && npm install && npm start

 Or run npm run build to create a Windows .exe.

In five years, we will know.

Many here point that Java didn’t kill anything, but Rust unlike Java has no GC.

It may take more than 5 years, Aketh. But Rust introduces a significant paradigm shift with its ownership and borrow checker system, which may potentially redefine how memory is managed and safety is ensured in software development.
If you do know max amount of data you are likely to push into the array, you could hardcode it as well

Would this be possible even without competing offers? 

Asking purely to try and understand how the market forces work in tech though.
Yup. Especially in a globalized world where it’s easy for people to misinterpret ours and everyone else”s behaviors and signals.
